pi_items.metadata_value: Real HBB sequence region (Human hemoglobin subunit beta, based on V00497.1) with true exon coordinates.
pi_items.verified_value: Real 1608 bp genomic fragment of human HBB (similar to NG_000007.3) with two single nucleotide variants compared to reference, but the provided coordinates are Coding Sequence (CDS) boundaries, not true exon boundaries.
pi_items.source: Analysis against NCBI NG_000007.3 genomic reference and V00497.1 mRNA. The provided 'exon' annotations (51..142, 273..495, 1346..1474) correspond to the CDS start/stop, omitting the 5' and 3' UTRs (true exons are 1..142, 273..495, 1346..1608).
Explicitly stated in the prompt context ('You are a Medical Geneticist...').
Explicitly stated in the prompt context ('...designing diagnostic primers for beta-thalassemia...').
Confirmed by reading export_result.sh and verifier.py logic.

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on a real 1608 bp genomic fragment of the human HBB gene (similar to NG_000007.3), but the metadata claim regarding 'true exon coordinates' is contradicted. The provided 'exon' annotations (51..142, 273..495, 1346..1474) strictly identify the Coding Sequence (CDS), omitting the 5' and 3' UTRs which are part of true exons 1 and 3. Despite this mislabeling, the resulting intron splice junctions (143..272 for Intron 1 and 496..1345 for Intron 2) accurately reflect the biologically true introns of human HBB because the UTRs do not cross splice boundaries. The expected files, including extracted FASTA sequences and a statistics report calculating exact length (130 bp and 850 bp) and GC content (~45.4% and ~30.9%), align perfectly with the script specifications.
